description: Phase 6 — Semantic Density: Fusion Generalizability. Same two themes as exp-14 (distributed systems expertise + compulsive boundary-tracing), but expressed in fresh wording. Tests whether the +123 fusion effect is a portable property of compound identity structure, or specific to the exp-14 sentence.
notes: Fusion Generalizability. A and B contain the same two themes as exp-14 A/B (distributed systems expertise + compulsive thoroughness about failure modes) but stated in entirely new wording. The variable is structural: A fuses both themes into one compound identity sentence; B splits them into two separate 'You are' anchors. If A > B (+~123 tokens): fusion is a portable structural property. If A ≈ B: the exp-14 fusion effect was sentence-specific — the specific tokens in that compound mattered, not the grammatical entanglement. C is the P_d floor anchor, identical to exp-14 C.
